conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by reading the official guidance on Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have three children. Alex is 5, Blake is 21, and Charlie is 13.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have three children - Alex (5), Blake (21), and Charlie (13).

Let me ask you some questions about each child to determine eligibility. I'll start with the basics:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your living arrangements with each child:

**Do all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) currently live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, all three of them live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now, regarding Blake who is 21 years old - Child Benefit typically only applies to children under 16, or under 20 if they're in approved education or training.

**Is Blake currently in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to check a few more things about the younger children:

**Are Alex (5) and Charlie (13) currently in hospital, in care, or in any residential accommodation paid for by the local council?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Charlie was in care for a little while recently, but Alex hasn't been in hospital or care at all.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. I need to understand more about Charlie's situation as this may affect eligibility.

**How long has Charlie been in care, and does Charlie spend any regular time at home each week (such as at least 24 hours per week, or 2 nights in a row each week)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Charlie was in care for about a month - 4 weeks to be exact. And no, Charlie wasn't coming home regularly during that time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One more important question about Charlie:

**Is Charlie still in care now, or has Charlie returned home to live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Charlie has returned home to live with me now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that clarification. Now I need to check one final important detail:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children - Alex, Blake, or Charlie?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, here's my assessment of your Child Benefit eligibility:

**Alex (5 years old): ✅ ELIGIBLE**
You can claim Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and meets all the eligibility criteria.

**Blake (21 years old): ❌ NOT ELIGIBLE**
Unfortunately, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Blake. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or under 20 if they're in approved education or training. Since Blake is 21 years old, they're over the age limit for Child Benefit.

**Charlie (13 years old): ✅ ELIGIBLE**
You can claim Child Benefit for Charlie. Charlie is under 16 and now lives with you at home. Although Charlie was in care for 4 weeks, this was under the 8-week threshold, and most importantly, Charlie has now returned home to live with you.

**Summary:** You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie, but not for Blake.

Please note: If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, though you can still claim the benefit.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 21 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 5 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Charlie
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 13 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 5 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and is not in hospital or care. You are responsible for Alex as the child lives with you. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

Blake is 21 years old. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or under 20 if they are in approved education or training. Since Blake is 21 years old, they are over the age limit for Child Benefit regardless of their education or training status. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 13 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and has returned home from care. Charlie was in care for 4 weeks, which is less than the 8-week threshold after which Child Benefit would normally stop (and Charlie wasn't spending at least 24 hours per week at home during that time). However, since Charlie has now returned home to live with you, you are responsible for Charlie again. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. Charlie meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit now that they have returned home.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with three children. Alex (5) and Charlie (13) both meet the eligibility criteria as they are under 16, live with the claimant, and no one else is claiming for them. Charlie's brief period in care (4 weeks) is noted but Charlie has now returned home. Blake (21) does not meet the eligibility criteria as they are over the age limit - Child Benefit only applies to children under 16, or under 20 if in approved education or training, and Blake is 21 and not in education or training.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 5 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and is not in hospital or care. You are responsible for Alex as the child lives with you. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

Blake is 21 years old. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or under 20 if they are in approved education or training. Since Blake is 21 years old, they are over the age limit for Child Benefit regardless of their education or training status. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 13 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and has returned home from care. Charlie was in care for 4 weeks, which is less than the 8-week threshold after which Child Benefit would normally stop (and Charlie wasn't spending at least 24 hours per week at home during that time). However, since Charlie has now returned home to live with you, you are responsible for Charlie again. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Charlie. Charlie meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit now that they have returned home.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with three children. Alex (5) and Charlie (13) both meet the eligibility criteria as they are under 16, live with the claimant, and no one else is claiming for them. Charlie's brief period in care (4 weeks) is noted but Charlie has now returned home. Blake (21) does not meet the eligibility criteria as they are over the age limit - Child Benefit only applies to children under 16, or under 20 if in approved education or training, and Blake is 21 and not in education or training.
